name: libpackage-variant-perl
title: libpackage-variant-perl
description:
parameterizable packages
Package::Variant allows you to build a variable package that contains a
package template and can use it to build variant packages at runtime.
.
Your variable package will export a subroutine which will build a
variant package, combining its arguments with the template, and return
the name of the new variant package.
.
The implementation does not care about what kind of packages it builds,
be they simple function exporters, classes, singletons or something
entirely different.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libpackage-variant-perl because parameterizable packages.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libpackage-variant-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libpackage-variant-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libpackage-variant-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
